title: iris pumila subsp. aequiloba
intro: Iris pumila subsp. aequiloba, also known as the dwarf iris, is a perennial plant that belongs to the iris family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of iris pumila subsp. aequiloba include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a diuretic, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, such as constipation and bloating, as well as its ability to help lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of kidney stones. The herbal preparations of iris pumila subsp. aequiloba include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
